sections.heading: Global Volume vs. Price/Mix Contribution

fields.label: Global unit case volume growth (total, by key region) (QoQ, YoY)

value.key_drivers: Growth across all global beverage categories, led by Trademark Coca-Cola and water, sports, coffee and tea. Coca-Cola Zero Sugar grew 13% across every geographic segment.
Growth in water, sports, coffee and tea, and Trademark Coca-Cola. Coca-Cola Zero Sugar expanded 13%.

value.total_global_volume: 33.8 billion unit cases sold, compared to 33.7 billion in 2024
value.regional_context: The United States represented 16% of worldwide volume, while Mexico, China, Brazil, and India collectively accounted for 33% of worldwide unit case volume. Sparkling soft drinks comprised 69% of worldwide unit case volume, with Trademark Coca-Cola alone accounting for 47%.

fields.label: Price/mix contribution to organic revenue growth (total, by key region) (QoQ, YoY)

value.emea_growth: Declined 3%, as pricing actions were more than offset by unfavorable mix

fields.label: Average selling price per unit case (trend, by region)
fields.value:
While explicit "average selling price per unit case" values are not consistently reported, Coca-Cola frequently reports "price/mix" as a key driver of organic revenue growth. Price/mix reflects the impact of pricing actions, the mix of products and packages sold, and the mix of channels and geographic territories.

Consolidated Price/Mix Growth:
*   +2% for Q1 2026 (driven by pricing actions and favorable mix).
*   +1% for Q4 2025 (driven by pricing actions and favorable mix).
*   +4% for Full Year 2025 (driven by pricing actions and favorable mix).
*   +6% for Q3 2025 (despite flat concentrate sales).

Regional Price/Mix Growth (Q1 2026):
*   Latin America: +1% (primarily driven by pricing actions, partially offset by unfavorable mix).
*   North America: +1% (primarily driven by pricing actions, partially offset by unfavorable mix).
*   Europe, Middle East & Africa (EMEA): -3% (pricing actions more than offset by unfavorable mix).
*   Asia Pacific: -6% (primarily driven by unfavorable mix and affordability initiatives).
*   Bottling Investments: -1% (primarily driven by unfavorable mix, partially offset by pricing actions).

Regional Price/Mix Growth (Q4 2025):
*   Europe, Middle East & Africa (EMEA): -3% (pricing actions more than offset by unfavorable mix).
*   Bottling Investments: +1% (driven by pricing actions, partially offset by unfavorable mix).
*   North America: +4% (primarily driven by pricing actions).

Historical Unit Revenue (Proxy for Average Selling Price):
*   $0.96 per case for 2023 (calculated from $26.539 billion revenue / 27.639 billion unit cases sold for Concentrate Operations).
*   Projection for 2026: Unit prices are anticipated to grow by +3.0%, largely driven by the Latin America and North America segments.

fields.label: SKU rationalization impact on volume/revenue

value.headline: Coca-Cola announced plans to cut its number of brands in half, reducing a portfolio of over 400 brands to roughly 200. This strategy was inspired by simplification efforts during the pandemic and aimed to improve operational execution, margins, and sales, with financial gains expected in later quarters.

The company's "fail fast" approach to new SKUs aims to avoid products with low sales, and this mindset has shown results, with revenue contribution from innovation being higher year-to-date and revenue per innovation doubling.
China's strong volume growth was attributed, in part, to portfolio rationalization and restaging efforts.
An Investor Presentation highlighted that a streamlined portfolio and disciplined innovation have tripled success rates compared to 2019, with increased speed and digital integration.

fields.label: Inflation pass-through rates (by key market)

value.details: Pricing actions were a primary driver for price/mix growth in Latin America (1% growth), EMEA (5% growth), and North America (5% growth). Coca-Cola Consolidated, a bottler, noted that increased aluminum costs in Q1 2026 'outpaced our pricing actions executed during the first quarter,' indicating efforts to pass through inflation, though not fully successful in this instance.

Pricing actions were a driver for the 1% global price/mix improvement. Latin America and North America saw 4% price/mix growth driven by pricing actions. In EMEA, pricing actions were taken but were more than offset by unfavorable mix, leading to a 3% decline in price/mix.

fields.label: Impact of packaging innovations on pricing

value.headline: The company adjusts its packaging mix in response to rising input costs, such as tariffs. For example, if aluminum cans become more expensive, Coca-Cola may emphasize PET bottles to maintain affordability.
The growth rate for "affordable and premium" returnable glass bottles (RGBs) in international markets outpaced total company volume growth, with 1.6 billion unit cases added in this format.

Coca-Cola is "fine-tuning pricing and packaging, mixing selective price increases with smaller, more affordable pack sizes to appeal to budget-sensitive consumers while managing geopolitical risks". The company uses different pack sizes, formats, and price points, ranging from smaller, lower-cost single-serve products to larger and premium items, to maintain affordability for budget-conscious shoppers.

Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings reported that wholesale price per case improved due to price revisions integrated with category and packaging strategies. However, vending machine (VM) volume was impacted by these price revisions, particularly for water and coffee, indicating a direct link between packaging-related price adjustments and consumer purchasing behavior.

The company's focus on sustainable packaging, including increasing recycled content and reducing material use, also has cost implications, with increased regulation potentially raising packaging costs.

fields.label: Competitive pricing actions in key markets

value.details: The Coca-Cola Company reported gaining value share in total nonalcoholic ready-to-drink (NARTD) beverages.

Value share gains were led by Trademark Coca-Cola, sparkling flavors, and water, sports, coffee and tea. The decline in Asia Pacific's price/mix was attributed to 'affordability initiatives', which could be a strategic response to competitive pricing or market conditions to maintain or gain share.
Value share gains in NARTD beverages were particularly strong in Argentina.

fields.label: Supply chain digitization project status & impact

value.value: The Coca-Cola Company announced the creation of a new Chief Digital Officer role to unify digital, data, and operational excellence across the company, with Sedef Salingan Sahin taking on the position, effective March 31, 2026.

As of September 2025, Coca-Cola is utilizing its Customer Demand & Supply Planning system, which incorporates machine learning to plan scenarios and generate sales demand forecasts. This aims to improve customer service and move towards a "factory of the future" model.

In September 2025, The Coca-Cola Company joined the newly established MIT Generative AI Impact Consortium as a founding member. This collaboration aims to explore how AI and other digital technologies can transform various aspects of its global business, including supply chain procurement.

As of February 2025, Coca-Cola is piloting an AI-driven initiative with its bottlers to send customized messages to customers with suggested items based on previous orders and market data. They also use a predictive algorithm for SKU suggestions, incorporating historical sales data, weather, and geotracking, which is being scaled to more customers and markets globally.

As of December 2024, Coca-Cola's expertise in supply chain logistics was leveraged to strengthen medical supply chains in Sierra Leone. Digital tools were implemented to improve inventory tracking at healthcare facilities and increase data visibility to the final mile.

In July 2022, a McKinsey report highlighted that Coca-Cola established a digital academy to upskill managers and frontline team leaders. This initiative led to a 10-20% improvement in productivity in manufacturing and enhanced service levels while reducing obsolescence across the supply chain. Additionally, in early 2021, the company built a digital twin of its manufacturing network to support business continuity planning and network optimization.

In 2021, Coca-Cola HBC implemented an SAP e-procurement solution in many markets (with further rollout planned for 2022) and successfully deployed SAP's S/4HANA to simplify processes and increase productivity across commercial, supply chain, and finance functions. Also in 2021, they introduced a new digital manufacturing platform with a monitoring system in seven markets, providing better insight into energy consumption and expanding predictive maintenance. Augmented reality headsets were introduced in warehouses to speed order packing and reduce error rates. Their business-to-business Customer Portal was transformed in 2020 into an engagement-driven digital experience, quadrupling its share of total orders to 8% in 2021.

In July 2021, Coca-Cola İçecek (CCI), a major bottler, modernized its manufacturing facility by creating a digital plant replica (digital twin) in the cloud. This project aimed to unlock value through advanced analytics, artificial intelligence (AI), and real-time asset monitoring. Within four months of deployment, CCI identified over 20 optimization points in equipment cleaning, reduced electricity usage by 20%, cut water usage by 9%, and added 34 days to its production schedule.

In November 2019, Coca-Cola announced its plan to incorporate blockchain technology into its supply chain, provided by SAP, for use at bottling companies. This permissioned business-grade blockchain was intended to make the supply chain more efficient by allowing authorized companies to file and view orders, with the potential to save billions of dollars. Initially, the system was for internal use, with consideration for expansion to retailers if successful.

By December 2018, Coca-Cola FEMSA, the largest Coca-Cola franchise bottler, completed a two-year supply chain transformation with JDA Software. This involved deploying a range of JDA solutions between March 2017 and July 2018 across Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Brazil, and Central America. The result was the ability to plan shipments in all 344 distribution centers with hourly tracking, improving customer service and maximizing process and cost efficiencies.

Coca-Cola generally utilizes various digital technologies to streamline its supply chain, including an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system, radio-frequency identification (RFID) tags to track product movement, and advanced analytics for predictive modeling to anticipate customer demand and optimize inventory.
